message:
test(golden_robot_json): scrub live watchdog counters + sampler snapshots to prevent flakes

The golden JSON test for `cass health --robot` compares scrubbed output
against a committed golden file. Two classes of field in the watchdog
section ticked between test runs and caused flakes:

  1. Integer counters that increment each sampler tick:
     `healthy_streak`, `ticks_total`, `load_window_len`,
     `psi_window_len`, `observations_total`. Depending on test-harness
     timing, the first assertion could see 0 ticks (sampler hasn't
     fired yet) or 1+ ticks (sampler has fired once during the output
     render). The golden was last updated against a run where the
     sampler had N ticks, so any other N caused a diff.

  2. `last_snapshot` + `last_reason` fields that switch shape entirely
     between runs: `null` (sampler has not fired) or an object/string
     (sampler fired and populated the snapshot). The object form has
     its inner floating-point fields already scrubbed by earlier rules,
     but the shape difference alone flipped the golden.

Fix: extend `scrub_robot_json(...)` with rules 7 and 8:

  - Rule 7 scrubs each integer counter key to the sentinel
    `"[LIVE_COUNTER]"`, so the golden pins the field's *presence and
    shape* without chasing the sampler timer.
  - Rule 8 folds both the null form and the populated-object form of
    `last_snapshot` to the single sentinel `"[LIVE_SAMPLE]"`, and
    similarly for `last_reason` (null or any string value). The object
    match uses `(?s)` multi-line regex to consume the pretty-printed
    JSON block up to the first `}`; kept narrow enough to fire only in
    the watchdog section.

Note on robustness: the scrubbed sentinel is a string, not a numeric
or null, so a parse-the-JSON-first golden would lose some type
fidelity here. We rely on the existing "scrub both sides, then
compare text" discipline in the test, which is why the narrow-regex
approach suffices — any false-positive collapse would still produce
a diff against the golden, so the scrub can only make the test less
strict, never less correct.

Complements the earlier float-scrubbing rule 6 and the timestamp /
path / UUID scrubbers above it. Consistent with how the existing
scrubber was already structured — each rule is a self-contained
regex block with its own doc-comment.

files.patch:
@@ -97,6 +97,61 @@ fn scrub_robot_json(input: &str, test_home: &std::path::Path) -> String {
             .to_string();
     }
 
+    // 7. Watchdog sampler counters in health --json. These tick each time
+    // the responsiveness sampler fires; the test can race with that timer
+    // (0 ticks before the first sample, 1+ ticks after). Scrub the integer
+    // to a placeholder so the golden locks the *shape* of the counter
+    // surface without chasing sampler-timing drift.
+    for key in [
+        "healthy_streak",
+        "ticks_total",
+        "load_window_len",
+        "psi_window_len",
+        "observations_total",
+    ] {
+        let re = regex::Regex::new(&format!(r#""{key}"\s*:\s*\d+"#)).unwrap();
+        out = re
+            .replace_all(&out, format!(r#""{key}": "[LIVE_COUNTER]""#).as_str())
+            .to_string();
+    }
+
+    // 8. `last_snapshot` + `last_reason` in health --json vary between
+    // `null` (sampler has not yet fired) and a populated object/string
+    // (sampler has fired at least once) depending on timing. The content
+    // of the populated form already has its inner floats scrubbed by
+    // rule 6; the remaining difference is whether the sampler fired. Fold
+    // both forms to a single sentinel so the golden does not race the
+    // sampler timer. We match `null`, a string value, or a `{...}` object
+    // by consuming everything up to the next unescaped `"..."` key at the
+    // same indentation — kept narrow so the scrub only fires on the
+    // health watchdog block.
+    //
+    // The object form is multi-line pretty-printed JSON; `(?s)` enables
+    // `.` to match newlines. Non-greedy match `.*?` stops at the first
+    // closing `}` on its own line at the correct indent. We rely on the
+    // outer scrub-then-compare discipline: any false-positive collapse
+    // would still fail the golden because the sentinel would differ
+    // between runs — the goal is deterministic scrubbing, not semantic
+    // parsing.
+    let last_snapshot_obj_re = regex::Regex::new(
+        r#"(?s)"last_snapshot"\s*:\s*\{[^}]*\}"#,
+    )
+    .unwrap();
+    out = last_snapshot_obj_re
+        .replace_all(&out, r#""last_snapshot": "[LIVE_SAMPLE]""#)
+        .to_string();
+    let last_snapshot_null_re =
+        regex::Regex::new(r#""last_snapshot"\s*:\s*null"#).unwrap();
+    out = last_snapshot_null_re
+        .replace_all(&out, r#""last_snapshot": "[LIVE_SAMPLE]""#)
+        .to_string();
+
+    let last_reason_re =
+        regex::Regex::new(r#""last_reason"\s*:\s*(null|"[^"]*")"#).unwrap();
+    out = last_reason_re
+        .replace_all(&out, r#""last_reason": "[LIVE_SAMPLE]""#)
+        .to_string();
+
     out
 }
